secondary_archetype: Creative Director / Head of Growth Creative

level_signals: 10+ years leading creative teams in performance marketing
at least 5 years managing multi-disciplinary teams at scale
Base comp range $194K–$245K depending on state, consistent with Director-level
Responsible for building and growing a multidisciplinary team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years leading creative teams in performance marketing, growth, or digital advertising, with at least 5 years managing multi-disciplinary teams at scale.
must_haves.qualifier: including at least 5 years managing multi-disciplinary teams at scale

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proven experience building and scaling large in-house creative organizations that support high-volume performance advertising.
Deep understanding of performance creative dynamics across Meta, TikTok, YouTube, Search, CRM, In-App and emerging channels, including how algorithms evaluate and reward creative.
Demonstrated ability to design workflows, systems, and processes that unlock speed, operational efficiency, and creative excellence.

Strong track record of integrating AI tools and automation platforms into creative production to increase throughput and reduce manual work, including a demonstrated ability to design workflows, systems, and processes that unlock speed, operational efficiency, and creative excellence.
Experience leading global creative operations, including localization strategies, regional workflows, and culturally relevant creative development.
Exceptional ability to translate performance insights into clear strategic creative direction that teams can execute against at high velocity.
Strong leadership, communication, and cross-functional partnership skills. Able to inspire teams, manage change, and create alignment across Growth, Product, Brand, and leadership.
Comfortable operating in fast-moving, experimental environments where volume, speed, and quality must coexist.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bonus points for experience in music, creator ecosystems, or high-growth consumer apps, though strategic depth and operational excellence matter most.
Bonus points for experience in food, grocery, advertising, or high-growth consumer apps, though strategic depth and operational excellence matter most.

scope.surface: Growth Creative / Local Produce (in-house creative agency) — paid and owned channels (media ads, email, SMS, web, digital, video, offline)
core_responsibilities: Build and lead a world-class creative team: Grow and mentor a multidisciplinary team across performance design and copy.
Own performance creative direction: Set the creative direction across Paid and CRM channels in partnership with marketing.
Drive AI-led transformation: Integrate AI and automation into every stage of the creative workflow.
Architect the creative engine for scale: Design workflows, pipelines, and systems that enable production of thousands of ads per week.
Scale creative globally: Build the systems and teams required to deliver locally relevant creative across international markets.
Partner with Growth leadership: Align creative strategy with media and analytics.
Champion collaboration and culture: Create an environment where creative teams feel empowered to test boldly, move quickly, and constantly raise the bar.

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant and annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation range fully disclosed across multiple US state tiers ($194K–$245K base)
Equity mentioned (new hire + annual refresh grants)
Fully remote role (#LI-Remote) with Flex First policy
Clear product surface and ownership area (Local Produce in-house agency)
Explicit AI-forward mandate signals investment in modern tooling
Opportunity to build a 'category-defining' function from senior leadership position
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for international candidates
Extremely high volume expectation ('thousands of ads per week') may indicate a high-pressure environment
10+ years required is a narrow filter for candidates earlier in their director career
JD lists 'music, creator ecosystems' as bonus in one bullet but omits it in the separate Preferred Qualifications section — possible copy inconsistency

fields_inferred_not_stated: scope.business_model (inferred from company description as B2C grocery delivery marketplace)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (ROI, CPA, CTR mentioned in JD body but not listed as direct KPIs for this role)
seniority.level (inferred from title, years required, and comp range)
compensation.base_range_usd lower bound taken from 'All other states' tier, upper bound from CA/NY/CT/NJ tier
location.work_auth and location.visa_sponsorship (not stated in JD)
ambiguities: Bonus/nice-to-have overlap: 'Bonus points for experience in music, creator ecosystems, or high-growth consumer apps' appears in Minimum Qualifications section, while a separate bullet in Preferred Qualifications says 'Bonus points for experience in food, grocery, advertising, or high-growth consumer apps' — the first bullet is placed under Minimum Qualifications but uses 'bonus points' language, treated as nice-to-have here.
Team reporting structure and size not stated — unclear whether this role has direct ICs, managers, or both.
International scope mentioned ('Scale creative globally', 'international markets') but no specific geographies named.
